checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 5's screenshot shows the file '2023_05_12_eager_nurse.jpg' selected with the delete option activated in the action bar; Step 6's screenshot displays the deletion confirmation dialog for this file, confirming the deletion action was initiated as required.

Step5 shows the target file selected with the Delete option activated in the action bar, matching the evidence hint of 'file selected with delete option activated'. Step6 initiates the deletion action by clicking the Delete button, triggering the confirmation dialog which aligns with the 'deletion prompt/confirmation dialog' evidence hint. checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 6's screenshot displays the system's deletion confirmation dialog for "2023_05_12_eager_nurse.jpg", and the agent clicked the "OK" button to confirm the deletion; Step 7's screenshot shows the "Deleting 1 item" message, verifying that the deletion action was initiated after confirmation.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 6 triggers the deletion confirmation dialog by clicking the Delete button on the selected file, and Step 7 confirms the deletion by clicking OK in the dialog, directly fulfilling the checkpoint's requirement of confirming the deletion action when prompted. These steps provide clear evidence of the confirmation interaction with the system's prompt.

checkpoint_results.evidence: The final screenshots (steps 8 and 9) of the Pictures folder within sdk_gphone64_arm64 storage still display a file with the truncated name '2023_05_12_ea...', which matches the prefix of the target file '2023_05_12_eager_nurse.jpg'.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 1 shows Markor's file browser interface with note_HrbOn.txt displayed in the file list; screenshot from step 2 shows note_HrbOn.txt opened in the editor, confirming the file was located and selected.

Step 2 shows selecting the note_HrbOn.txt file from Markor's file list, confirming the file was successfully located, which directly meets the checkpoint's requirement of having the file selected/displayed in Markor's file list.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 2 shows note_HrbOn.txt's content ('Beauty is in the eye of the beholder.') visible, with a red cursor present at the top-left, confirming editing capabilities are active.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 1 launches the Markor app to access the file browser interface, which is the prerequisite to locate the target note. Step 2 clicks on note_HrbOn.txt, and the observation confirms the note opened in Markor's editor with its content visible, directly demonstrating that the note is in edit mode with editing capabilities active, satisfying the checkpoint's requirement.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 2's after screenshot shows the cursor positioned on the line below the existing note text 'Beauty is in the eye of the beholder.', not at the very start before any existing text. Step 3's after screenshot confirms the added text 'Inconceivable!' appears below the original text, aligning with the cursor being placed after the existing content.
checkpoint_results.missing_or_conflict: The cursor was not positioned at the very start of the note's content before any existing text; instead, it was placed after the original text, leading to the new text being added below rather than at the top.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 2 opens the target note in Markor's editor, and its after screenshot shows the note content with the cursor positioned at the very start of the existing text. Step 3's before screenshot confirms this cursor position before the text 'Inconceivable!' is added, which directly aligns with the checkpoint's requirement of placing the cursor at the start of the note's content.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 3's after screenshot shows 'Inconceivable!' appears as the second line of note_HrbOn.txt, below the original line 'Beauty is in the eye of the beholder.'
checkpoint_results.missing_or_conflict: The text 'Inconceivable!' was not placed at the top of the note as required; instead, it is positioned below the original content, conflicting with the checkpoint's requirement to add it to the top.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 2 opens note_HrbOn.txt and shows its original content without "Inconceivable!". Step 3 records the action of typing "Inconceivable!" into the note, and its summary confirms the text is now the first line of the note, directly matching the checkpoint's requirement of having the text at the top/cursor position. Both steps have before/after screenshots to support the evidence.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 4 shows the save button was clicked and disabled, indicating the save action was attempted; the note displays the updated content while still open (steps 3-5).
